summary: Compares approaches to executive aggrandizement across systems, analyzing weakening checks, court-packing, media control.
key_points: Different countries adopt varying approaches to executive aggrandizement.
Comparative analysis reveals: weakening checks, court-packing, media control.
Institutional context shapes how this factor operates in practice.
Reform efforts draw on lessons from other jurisdictions.
No single model dominates; contextual fit matters.
